name: Valdeolea Menhirs Trail
name_original: Ruta de los Menhires de Valdeolea (PR-S 61) — circular desde Mataporquera
description: Hike the 7.8-mile (12.6 km) Route of the Menhires of Valdeolea, a moderate circular trail from Mataporquera, exploring ancient monoliths.
region_intro: Hiking around Valdeolea offers diverse landscapes characterized by mountainous terrain, narrow valleys, and stretching peaks. The region features lush green scenery, combining woods and open spaces, with traditional stone huts adding to the rustic environment. This area provides a range of trails suitable for various fitness levels, allowing exploration of its natural and historical features. Best hiking trails around Valdeolea The most popular hiking route is Salcedillo, Puente Rojadillo, and Chozo Tonino Trail, a 5.5 miles (8.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route guides hikers through historical sites and varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Valdeolea Menhirs Trail, a moderate 7.8 miles (12.6 km) path. This trail leads past ancient megalithic complexes, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical intrigue. Local hikers also love the Julióbriga Roman City – circular from Bolmir via La Llanuca, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through historical Roman sites, often completed in about 1 hour 11 minutes. Hiking around Valdeolea is defined by mountainous terrain, narrow valleys, and lush green woodlands. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Valdeolea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Valdeolea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails are available in Valdeolea?
A: Valdeolea offers a diverse range of hiking trails suitable for various fitness levels. You'll find everything from easy, short walks to more challenging moderate routes. The landscape is characterized by mountainous terrain, narrow valleys, and lush green scenery, often featuring traditional stone huts and ancient historical sites.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Valdeolea has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998339732/juliobriga-roman-city-circular-from-bolmir-via-la-llanuca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Julióbriga Roman City – circular from Bolmir via La Llanuca&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.4 km long and takes around 1 hour 11 minutes. This route allows you to explore historical Roman sites without a strenuous climb.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Valdeolea?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Valdeolea are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103997582/salcedillo-puente-rojadillo-and-chozo-tonino-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salcedillo, Puente Rojadillo, and Chozo Tonino Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.9 km loop guiding hikers through historical sites and varied landscapes.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I discover while hiking in Valdeolea?
A: Valdeolea is rich in both natural and historical landmarks. You can explore ancient megalithic complexes, such as those found along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176602052/valdeolea-menhirs-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeolea Menhirs Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which features 'timeless sentinels' of stone. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3672317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Julióbriga&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5648200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corvio Necropolis&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter traditional stone huts scattered across the slopes, adding to the rustic charm.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to viewpoints with scenic vistas?
A: While specific viewpoint trails aren't detailed, the mountainous terrain of Valdeolea inherently offers breathtaking vistas. The region is known for its lush green scenery, combining woods and open spaces, with horizons of stretching peaks. Many trails will naturally provide expansive views of the valleys and surrounding mountain ranges.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Valdeolea?
A: The trails in Valdeolea are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the sense of untouched nature, and the blend of natural beauty with historical intrigue found along the routes.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in the Valdeolea area?
A: There are over 80 hiking routes available for exploration in the Valdeolea area, catering to a wide range of preferences and skill levels. This includes 25 easy routes, 54 moderate routes, and a few more challenging options.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Valdeolea?
A: Valdeolea, benefiting from the broader Cantabrian environment, is generally lush and green. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, making them ideal for hiking. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, while winter might offer a different, more serene experience, though some higher routes could be affected by weather.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique geological features to see?
A: Yes, Valdeolea is home to several interesting natural features. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2573626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontibre&lt;/a&gt;, which are significant natural monuments. The region's mountainous terrain and narrow valleys also contribute to its unique geological character.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Valdeolea?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Valdeolea, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pets on trails.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes in Valdeolea?
A: While Valdeolea has a good selection of easy and moderate trails, there are also a few more challenging options for experienced hikers. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092479236/memoria-romanica-palentina-circular-desde-cillamayor" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Memoria románica palentina — circular desde Cillamayor&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route spanning over 15 km, offering a longer day out in the landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on the trails?
A: You should expect varied terrain in Valdeolea. Trails often traverse mountainous areas with narrow valleys, lush green woodlands, and open spaces. Some paths might be rocky or uneven, while others could be smoother. The presence of traditional stone huts and ancient sites adds to the unique character of the paths.
tips: Menhir 3.86m high that offers panoramic views of the valley and its surroundings. It is known by this name because, prior to its restoration, it was so inclined that it looked like a canyon.
It is 4 meters high, of which 2.75 meters are visible, and weighs approximately 4.3 tons.

It is the largest of those found in Valdeolea and one of the largest in Spain, with a total height of 4.85 meters, of which 3.85 meters are visible, and an estimated weight of 5 tons. Before its restoration it was divided into two halves. It has engravings on both sides.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Route of the Menhires of Valdeolea (PR-S 61)?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While it requires a good physical condition and steady footing, the paths are largely accessible. It's suitable for hikers with some experience, offering a rewarding journey without extreme challenges.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features diverse terrain, including unpaved tracks, paved tracks, and some asphalt roads. You'll walk through fields, meadows, and pine forests, providing varied scenery throughout the hike. Some sections might be off known paths, adding to the adventure.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Route of the Menhires?
faqs.answer: The primary attractions are the ancient Neolithic menhirs. You'll encounter impressive monoliths such as the Piedrahita Menhir or 'El Carón', the Menhir El Peñuco de Bercedo, the monumental Menhir El Cabezudo, and the Menhir de la Llaneda. These ancient stones offer a unique glimpse into prehistoric times.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating and approximately 12.6 km length, the trail is best suited for those with some hiking experience. While not overly strenuous, beginners might find the duration and varied terrain challenging. Families with younger children might prefer shorter, easier routes.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on the Route of the Menhires?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Route of the Menhires of Valdeolea (PR-S 61) is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near livestock, and to ensure you clean up after them.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Valdeolea region is particularly beautiful in spring, when the landscape is lush and green, often referred to as 'Spanish Tuscany'. Autumn also offers pleasant hiking conditions with cooler temperatures and vibrant colors. Summers can be warm, so an early start is advisable, while winter hikes might require checking weather conditions for snow or ice.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Route of the Menhires of Valdeolea (PR-S 61). It's a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Mataporquera?
faqs.answer: As the trail is a circular route starting from Mataporquera, you can typically find parking within the village itself. Look for designated parking areas or suitable street parking close to the start of the PR-S 61 route.

faqs.question: Are there any public transport options to reach the trailhead in Mataporquera?
faqs.answer: Mataporquera is a small village, and public transport options might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or train connections to Mataporquera from larger nearby towns if you plan to rely on public transport to reach the trailhead.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other trails. You will find parts of the Ruta de los Menhires, as well as smaller segments of the Camino Olvidado (Bimón-Matamorisca) and the Camino del Besaya: Cantabria.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers delightful scenery with panoramic views of the Valdeolea valley and surrounding mountains. You'll pass through broad valleys, fields, meadows, and pine forests, providing a picturesque backdrop to the ancient menhirs.
